data.title: Kraken Launches Aptos Native USDT Deposits and Withdrawals
data.description: Kraken now supports deposits and withdrawals of Aptos' native USDT and has designated Aptos as its preferred network for USDT. Since its launch on the Aptos mainnet in late 2024, USDT supply has increased eightfold, and Aptos currently ranks second among the most active networks supporting USDT.

data.title: Binance Futures platform has now returned to normal
data.description: The Binance Futures platform has now returned to normal, and fund transfers and other operations can be processed normally.

data.title: Investor Faces Significant Losses Despite Holding Large Positions
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, recent monitoring by MLM indicates that investor Huang Licheng has experienced substantial financial setbacks. After initially gaining $44 million on September 19, his account has now nearly returned all profits, leaving only $1 million in gains.

Over a span of 20 days, Huang incurred a floating loss of $42 million. Despite these losses, he continues to hold long positions valued at approximately $150 million, with an overall leverage of 14.7 times.

data.title: Over the past hour, the entire network has experienced a liquidation of over $70 million, mainly due to long orders.
data.description: Coinglass data shows that in the past hour, the entire network had a liquidation of US$71.4623 million, of which long positions had a liquidation of US$67.1258 million and short positions had a liquidation of US$4.3366 million.

data.title: U.S. Senate Rejects Funding Plans Amid Government Shutdown
data.description:
According to PANews, the U.S. Senate rejected two funding proposals on Wednesday aimed at ending the nine-day government shutdown, leaving the cryptocurrency policy agenda in limbo due to unresolved budget issues. Despite this, insiders in Washington believe that momentum has not been entirely lost. While the House of Representatives has paused its activities due to the shutdown, the Senate continues to meet on budget and cryptocurrency-related matters.

Two informed sources revealed that the Senate Banking Committee is determined to advance cryptocurrency market structure policies, even during the shutdown. The leadership aims to complete revisions to the Responsible Financial Innovation Act by the end of the month. In July, the House passed the Financial Transparency Act, after which the Senate Banking and Agriculture Committees each drafted their own bills, which will eventually be merged. Last month, the Banking Committee's Republicans released a revised version, while Democrats provided a list of principles they hope to see included in the legislation. The Agriculture Committee's draft has yet to be released and may take longer to finalize.

During the shutdown, most personnel collaborating with Congress have been laid off, and new spot cryptocurrency ETFs are unlikely to be approved until the shutdown ends. However, issuers are optimistic that new products will be swiftly approved once the government resumes operations, despite not having received any updates from the SEC during the shutdown.

data.title: Bybit receives Virtual Asset Platform Operator license from the UAE Securities and Commodities Authority
data.description: Bybit announced that it has been granted a Virtual Asset Platform Operator license by the Securities and Commodities Authority (SCA) of the United Arab Emirates. This license will enable Bybit to provide regulated virtual asset trading, brokerage, custody, and fiat currency conversion services to retail and institutional clients throughout the UAE.

data.title: Citigroup: Core inflation is expected to cool down in September
data.description: Citigroup economists expect the core CPI to rise 0.28% in September, down from 0.35% in August. While tariffs are likely to keep commodity prices firm, slower housing inflation should moderate overall services inflation. The government shutdown could delay the release of data, but Citigroup said a softening labor market and cooling house prices reduce the risk of persistent inflation.

data.title: Trump expresses regret over 'horrible' government shutdown: House Speaker
data.description: U.S. House Speaker Johnson said that U.S. President Trump called him last night after the peace agreement was reached and expressed regret for the "horrible" shutdown situation. (Jinshi)

data.title: Du Jun Criticizes Shorting Ethereum Amid Market Trends
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, ABCDE co-founder Du Jun expressed on social media that while shorting premium treasury companies like BMNR is feasible, shorting Ethereum would be unwise.

Previously, BlockBeats reported that the well-known short-selling firm Kerrisdale Capital announced it had shorted the stock of Ethereum treasury reserve company Bitmine. Kerrisdale Capital argued that the Digital Asset Treasury (DAT) model has become mundane and lacks innovation. As nearly identical projects flood the market, premiums are collapsing, and the ability to increase the per-share Ethereum content by issuing stock above net asset value (NAV) is diminishing.

Companies pursuing the "crypto asset treasury strategy" have collectively aimed to raise over $100 billion, undermining the scarcity that once supported high premiums. Currently, many of these companies' stocks are trading at or below their net value, with their self-sustaining mechanisms stalling and models beginning to collapse.

data.title: Kerrisdale Capital clarifies: No short position in ETH
data.description: Short-selling firm Kerrisdale Capital previously announced a short position on Ethereum treasury firm BitMine. However, the company clarified on the X platform that it did not short ETH and stated that anyone interested in ETH could simply purchase it directly. Meanwhile, according to US stock market data, BMNR fell 3.55% during trading, bringing its market capitalization down to $16.5 billion.
